title: Content richness lane
summary: Twenty-ninth structured packet for Eco Explorer. Lane 2 is a parallel content-richness lane that deepens the world through authored discovery packs and journal-facing richness while staying mostly inside `src/content/**` and existing surfaces. The lane should strengthen the coastal front, then the alpine half, then expand shared-species comparisons and close-look candidates without reopening route or station systems.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the coastal-front content richness pack.
execution_order.why_now: The coast is the safest place to add immediate density without colliding with the systems lane.

execution_order.goal: Implement the coastal-front content richness pack.
execution_order.why_now: The front half of the world benefits most from additional authored density right now.

execution_order.goal: Review the coastal-front content richness pack.
execution_order.why_now: Science accuracy and readability stay the main gate for a content-only lane.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the alpine content richness pack.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline and tundra can deepen in parallel without stepping on the route-board lane.

execution_order.goal: Implement the alpine content richness pack.
execution_order.why_now: The inland half should gain more authored richness after the coastal-front pass proves the lane.

execution_order.goal: Review the alpine content richness pack.
execution_order.why_now: The lane should only continue if it stays science-safe and calm.

execution_order.goal: Prepare comparison and close-look expansion candidates.
execution_order.why_now: Once the world is denser, the journal can become more rewarding without new systems.

execution_order.goal: Expand shared-species comparisons and close-look content.
execution_order.why_now: This gives the content lane a high-signal journal payoff using systems already in place.

execution_order.goal: Review the comparison and close-look expansion.
execution_order.why_now: The lane should end with a quality gate before any next content wave is queued.

execution_order.goal: Add one small sketchbook or atlas richness follow-up after the content wave proves out.
execution_order.why_now: A tiny payoff pass helps lane 2 capitalize on new density without opening another system.

guardrails: Prefer `src/content/**`, science docs, and content-focused tests.
Avoid route-board, station-shell, and wider progression changes.
Use existing comparison, note, close-look, and sketchbook systems instead of inventing new ones.

During the coastal-front pass, add source-backed discoveries and notes first; defer comparison allowlist and close-look payload expansion to the later journal-richness step unless a new authored entry must be seeded now.
During the alpine pass, prioritize one shared alpine anchor plus one treeline and one tundra-side density beat; avoid reopening field-request, route, or expedition logic.
Keep additions science-safe, kid-readable, and visually calm.
acceptance_focus: The world feels denser and more collectible without system drift.
Lane 2 stays mostly inside content-owned files.
Journal-facing richness improves without new UI or progression layers.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-2.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-content-richness-lane.md, and this packet. Treat lane 2 as a content-owned parallel lane. Deepen the world through authored discovery packs, note richness, comparisons, and close-look content while avoiding route-board and station-shell work.
